A Fine Arts foundation, put to work on construction sites and client brand systems alike.

I've spent a decade designing brand identity, print, and digital assets for freelance clients, while building an equally strong specialty in PowerPoint and executive presentation design inside a corporate, deadline-driven environment. That combination means I design work that isn't just beautiful — it communicates clearly, follows a structure, and holds up in front of decision-makers.

01 — Graphic Design Work

Brand identity & campaign design

Selected projects from a decade of freelance practice — from architectural brand marks rooted in Cairo's heritage, to full print campaigns for medical conferences.

Brand Identity

Sakakini Palace

Cairo, Egypt

Sakakini Palace is a masterpiece of architectural eclecticism, yet its intricate details are often lost in complex photographs. The goal was to capture the grand, awe-inspiring feeling of looking up at the central dome — distilling that heavy, historical architecture into a minimal, scalable, modern geometric logo without losing its cultural soul.

Architecture to identity: the Sakakini dome

Brand Identity

El Moez Street

Islamic Cairo, Egypt

El Moez Street carries immense historical and visual weight. Rather than a generic icon or a literal drawing of a building, the challenge was to design a custom typographic logomark — where the Arabic letters themselves reflect the verticality and structural elegance of the street.

Print & Campaign

Precision Oncology Conference

6th Annual Edition

A full print campaign system for a medical conference — built to stay consistent from a stage backdrop down to a delegate's name tag.

BrandingFlyerBackdropID / Name TagProgram Book
Print & Campaign

The Evolving Landscape of Breast Cancer Care

Medical Conference Campaign

A companion campaign built on the same system logic — a coherent identity carried across every printed touchpoint of the event.
